document.name: Step Therapy Requirements – Biologic Therapy for Severe Asthma

step_therapy.description: Step therapy is required prior to approval of biologic therapy unless an exception applies.

steps.documentation_required: Prescription records, pharmacy claims, or chart notes confirming dose and duration

steps.description: Trial of at least one additional controller medication
steps.documentation_required: Prescription records showing trial of at least one additional controller

step_therapy.combination_therapy_note: ICS/LABA combination inhalers (e.g., Advair, Symbicort, Breo) satisfy both Step 1 and Step 2 simultaneously if used at high-dose ICS equivalent for ≥90 days.

failure_criteria.description: Step therapy is considered failed when any of the following occur despite adequate trial duration at appropriate doses.

indicators.description: ≥2 asthma exacerbations requiring systemic corticosteroids within the trial period
≥1 hospitalization or emergency department visit for asthma during the trial period
Persistent uncontrolled symptoms as measured by validated instrument (ACT score <20 or ACQ score >1.5)
Requirement for daily or near-daily rescue inhaler use (>2 days/week)

phenotype_guidance.description: Biologic selection should be guided by asthma phenotype when step therapy has been completed.

phenotype_to_biologic_mapping.biomarker: Blood eosinophils ≥150 cells/µL at initiation or ≥300 cells/µL in prior 12 months
